tournaments.title: Mauritzberg
tournaments.recap:
**It all started with a vision.**

A vision of something permanent through time, a tradition to cherish and uphold. A golf tournament not only about the game, but about the friendship and atmosphere surrounding it. A well-needed excuse to pause our everyday lives and the hustle and bustle that comes with it. A chance to get away for a few days and only focus on two things: the game of golf, and having a great time.

In the beginning they were four. Four friends collected from different eras of a man's life. Good friends, standing the test of time. The invitation was for two rounds of golf with a one-night overlay, but on the driving range a reveal was made. This wasn't just a few games of recreational golf — this was a tournament. Small in scale, but bold in concept. There were rules and prizes, a custom-made Winners Hat, and a name for the ages: **Condor Gilligan Invitational**.

The first edition of this tournament went on to be a raging success. On the field, the very much occasional golfer Calle impressed with his irons, and combined with a lot of extra strokes he proved hard to beat. Outside the course we enjoyed the classy facilities of Mauritzberg Manor — a dinner in one of the wings followed by a late night by the grand piano singing Kent and Oasis.

tournaments.title: Norrköping / Villa Baro
tournaments.notes: Sebastian Ahlström walked over due to sickness.
tournaments.recap:
**The second year — the start of a tradition and the year of the Bao.**

We started with a night in Norrköping. People arrived just in time for dinner and we met up in the hotel bar. We headed out to The Lamp, supposed to be one of the best restaurants in town. The evening was a bit chill but we sat outside in the courtyard anyway. A lot of small courses were ordered, and some of us decided to go for the variations of bao buns that the menu had to offer. Not all of us were satisfied, to put it mildly — Anthony has not stopped talking about these very baos to this day and is apparently scarred for life. We hope you will get over it someday.

Early next morning we headed out to Norrköping/Söderköping Golf Club for the first round of the tournament. Not much is remembered from this round, but following up on his success from last year, Carl came out on top. Afterwards we headed for our accommodation at Villa Baro where we swiftly changed into bathrobes and headed for the spa area and some sauna. A great dinner followed, and the night ended with a game of pool and some drinks.

The next morning we played the golf course connected to the hotel, beautifully located by the lake, starting and finishing just metres from our rooms. It was a beautiful day, but in time for the last couple of holes a rainstorm swooped in, making the finish a real struggle. Soaked as we were, we headed straight to the changing rooms where Anthony, living up to being the pre-tournament favourite, received the Winners Hat from Calle to make his win official.

All in all, a great event!

tournaments.title: Eskilstuna
tournaments.recap:
**A house by the water.**

For the first time in CGI history we traded the hotels and golf resorts for an Airbnb! A cozy house by the water a few miles outside Eskilstuna, complete with a sauna, large patio and barbecue. For the first time we also stayed for 3 nights, arriving on Thursday, playing Friday–Saturday, and ending with a grand dinner Saturday night before heading back on Sunday morning. This new setup was much approved and will most likely make a comeback in the future.

The first round was played at Eskilstuna GK in weather more like what you'd find in early April. 10 degrees and heavy rain forced the players to use some willpower to make it back to the club house. The debutant Oscar impressed during the round but fell short at the end. A highlight was the last hole where we had the closest-to-pin contest, a par 3 over the water, overlooked by the club house patio where the first group could heckle the second one as they made their attempts to hit the green.

The second round was played at Strand GK. A course with a bit more ambition than the previous one and nice and welcoming facilities. The weather was better now even if the temperature was still a bit low for the season. Tony came out strong with an early birdie on the second hole, but out of nowhere Fredrik wins both closest and longest (probably the first points ever in these categories for him) and despite having to endure 3 gilligans on the 17th he manages to catch his first win.

The 5th edition of the tournament was thereby over, and at the closing dinner custom-made 5-year patches were awarded to the few players who had participated in all tournaments — a tradition that hopefully will continue going forward.

tournaments.title: Winter Edition — Stockholm
tournaments.recap:
**The first CGI Winter Edition.**

The off-season is long in Sweden. Unbearably long. Luckily there are ways to at least dampen the longing and scratch the itch even when it is snowing sideways and the courses are closed.

4 hours of simulator golf goes a long way — no walking, no searching for lost balls, no weather and no waiting for other groups. We took the opportunity and played a course in Canada, a fairly easy one but hard enough for the rusty winter golfers. We also did stroke play by mistake, adding some suspense throughout the round, but it also made it possible to go +5 on the first hole as I myself had to suffer through. The fact that we were not scattered along a golf course also enabled us to have standalone longest- and closest competitions where Erik proved hard to beat.

After a nice time at RUFF we headed for the city for a well-deserved dinner. We ate delicious but rather spicy food at Surfers and then ended the night at the legendary rock bar Pub Anchor, where most of us had a hard time blending in with the regulars.

More Winter Editions to come!
